medications.indication_rationale: Group-specific pulmonary hypertension therapy (e.g. pulmonary vasodilators for pulmonary arterial hypertension) is specialist-initiated only after diagnostic characterisation (echocardiography/right heart catheterisation); it must not be started over telehealth and can be harmful in the wrong PH group.
contraindications_in_this_case: Initiate only via a pulmonary hypertension specialist after diagnostic work-up - not remotely

medications.indication_rationale: Cautious diuresis may be used for right-heart congestion but requires in-person assessment and monitoring (over-diuresis reduces RV preload and cardiac output); it should not be initiated remotely.
contraindications_in_this_case: Initiate/titrate only with in-person assessment and monitoring - not over telehealth

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Continuing usual activity / 'pushing through' the breathlessness
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Exertion in pulmonary hypertension with exertional syncope can precipitate a syncopal collapse or sudden death; strict activity restriction is required until assessed.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Strict activity restriction: pace all activities well below the point of breathlessness or dizziness, and avoid exertion until urgently assessed.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Exertion can trigger syncope or a dangerous fall in cardiac output; limiting it reduces the immediate sudden-death/collapse risk.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep the legs elevated when sitting, rest frequently, and watch for chest pain, severe breathlessness at rest, or fainting.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Leg elevation eases dependent oedema, and these red flags would require an immediate emergency response.

patient_education_points.point: The blood vessels in your lungs appear to have become narrowed/stiff, raising the pressure and straining the right side of your heart - that's causing your breathlessness, the swelling, and importantly the near-fainting on walking. Fainting on exertion is a warning sign that needs urgent assessment.

Please arrange urgent in-person assessment and an urgent heart scan (echocardiogram). Until then, rest strictly - don't push through the breathlessness or exert yourself, because that can bring on a faint.
Call 000 for an ambulance if you get chest pain, become severely breathless at rest, or actually faint.
This isn't simply being unfit - it needs specialist heart/lung assessment, and there are specific treatments once the exact cause and pressures are measured.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day/next-day in-person assessment with urgent echocardiography; GP review within 24-48 hours and expedited respiratory/cardiology referral for right heart catheterisation
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Echocardiographic pulmonary pressures and RV function, oxygenation, natriuretic peptides/renal function, the underlying cause of pulmonary hypertension (including chronic thromboembolic disease), and specialist group-specific therapy and activity guidance
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Chest pain, severe breathlessness at rest, or an actual faint - call 000 immediately.

must_include_items: Recognition of likely pulmonary hypertension/right heart failure (right-sided signs, not deconditioning)
Heavy weighting of exertional syncope as a high-risk (sudden-death) marker
Strict activity restriction until urgently assessed
Urgent in-person assessment with urgent echocardiography and specialist referral
Safety-netting to call 000 for chest pain, severe rest breathlessness, or an actual faint
acceptable_alternatives: Urgent same-day ED/acute assessment or urgent (within 24-48h) cardiology/respiratory review with urgent echo are acceptable given the exertional-syncope risk; 'high pressure in the lung arteries straining the right heart', 'pulmonary hypertension' are acceptable framings
errors_of_omission: Attributing to deconditioning/ageing with no escalation
Not weighting exertional syncope / not advising strict rest
No safety-netting for syncope/rest breathlessness/chest pain
errors_of_commission: Starting pulmonary vasodilators or unmonitored diuretics remotely
Mislabelling as asthma/COPD and giving bronchodilators
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: HIGH-ACUITY CASE: A pass requires (1) recognising likely pulmonary hypertension/right heart failure and weighting the exertional syncope, (2) strict activity restriction, and (3) urgent in-person assessment with urgent echocardiography/specialist referral. Reassuring about deconditioning, allowing continued exertion, or arranging only routine follow-up is a dangerous output given the sudden-death risk.
